title: ResolutionNode in evaluation graph
analysis: The evaluation graph currently has 3 node types (ValueCell, Constraint, Realization) with a NodeId enum in cache.rs that unifies them. Task 20 adds a 4th: ResolutionNode. ResolutionNodeId already exists in reify-types/src/identity.rs (entity: String, index: u32). NodeId is defined in crates/reify-eval/src/cache.rs (NOT reify-types/identity.rs as the task description suggests). The NodeId enum is used in: cache.rs (definition + From impls + CachedResult content_hash), deps.rs (ReverseDependencyIndex build_from_graph, build_trace_map), dirty.rs (compute_dirty_cone match on NodeId::Value for propagation), demand.rs (rebuild_cone with explicit match arms per variant), lib.rs (Engine eval/edit_param constructing NodeIds), and runtime/lib.rs (test-only NodeId construction, no match arms). Key patterns: (1) NodeId has From<T> impls for each ID type; (2) CachedResult has domain-separated content_hash with tag bytes [20],[21],[22]; (3) dirty cone BFS only propagates through Value nodes (others are leaf); (4) demand cone BFS has explicit match arms extracting deps per node type; (5) topology_fingerprint sorts hashes per-type then combines in fixed order; (6) build_from_graph and build_trace_map iterate all graph maps explicitly. The from_templates() method currently processes value_cells, constraints, and realizations from TopologyTemplate. TopologyTemplate has no resolutions field yet, and adding it is out of scope for this task (requires compiler changes). ResolutionNodes will be constructed manually in tests. The demand cone match in demand.rs needs a new arm for Resolution that returns auto_params as ValueCellId reads. Dirty cone propagation treats Resolution nodes as leaf nodes (like Constraint/Realization) since the only propagation is through Value nodes.

prerequisites.description: Verify existing tests pass with `cargo test -p reify-eval` before making any changes

steps.description: Write failing test in graph.rs: `resolution_node_data_construction`. Create a ResolutionNodeData with id=ResolutionNodeId::new('Bracket', 0), scope='Bracket', auto_params=[ValueCellId::new('Bracket','x')], constraint_deps=[ConstraintNodeId::new('Bracket',0)], content_hash=ContentHash::of_str('res0'). Assert all fields. Also test Debug and Clone derives. Fails because ResolutionNodeData doesn't exist.

Add ResolutionNodeData struct to graph.rs: `pub struct ResolutionNodeData { pub id: ResolutionNodeId, pub scope: String, pub auto_params: Vec<ValueCellId>, pub constraint_deps: Vec<ConstraintNodeId>, pub content_hash: ContentHash }` with #[derive(Debug, Clone)]. Add ResolutionNodeId to imports from reify_types.

Write failing test in graph.rs: `evaluation_graph_has_resolutions_map`. Create EvaluationGraph::default(), assert graph.resolutions.is_empty() and graph.resolutions.len() == 0. Fails because resolutions field doesn't exist on EvaluationGraph.

Add `pub resolutions: PersistentMap<ResolutionNodeId, ResolutionNodeData>` field to EvaluationGraph struct. The existing #[derive(Default)] handles initialization to empty. Update the existing `evaluation_graph_empty` test to also assert resolutions.is_empty().

Write failing test in cache.rs: `node_id_resolution_variant`. Create NodeId::Resolution(ResolutionNodeId::new('A', 0)), assert it equals itself, differs from NodeId::Value/Constraint/Realization. Test From<ResolutionNodeId> for NodeId conversion. Fails because NodeId has no Resolution variant.

Add Resolution(ResolutionNodeId) variant to NodeId enum in cache.rs. Add `impl From<ResolutionNodeId> for NodeId`. Add ResolutionNodeId to the reify_types use statement. Fix all match-arm exhaustiveness errors: (1) demand.rs rebuild_cone: add `NodeId::Resolution(res_id) => { ... }` arm that looks up graph.resolutions.get(res_id) and returns auto_params as reads; (2) dirty.rs compute_dirty_cone: no change needed (only matches NodeId::Value for propagation, others fall through); (3) topological_sort: no change needed (only matches NodeId::Value(ref vcid) for edge propagation).

Write failing test in cache.rs: `cached_result_resolution_variant`. Create CachedResult::Resolution with HashMap{ValueCellId::new('A','x') => Value::Real(1.0)}. Call content_hash() and assert non-zero, uses tag [23]. Create a second with different values, assert different hash. Fails because CachedResult has no Resolution variant.

Add `Resolution(HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>)` variant to CachedResult enum. Add match arm in content_hash(): tag [23], sort entries by ValueCellId Debug repr for determinism, combine key and value hashes. Ensure HashMap is imported (std::collections::HashMap already imported at top of cache.rs).

Write test in cache.rs: `cache_store_resolution_result`. Create CacheStore, record_evaluation with NodeId::Resolution(ResolutionNodeId::new('A',0)) and CachedResult::Resolution(HashMap), verify get() returns the entry, freshness is Final, result_hash matches content_hash.

Verify test passes — CacheStore is generic over NodeId/CachedResult variants. Fix any issues. This step should be a no-op since the existing CacheStore HashMap<NodeId, NodeCache> handles any NodeId variant.

Write failing test in deps.rs: `reverse_index_includes_resolution_deps`. Build EvaluationGraph manually with: param 'a' (auto), constraint C0, and ResolutionNodeData R0 with auto_params=['a']. Call build_from_graph. Assert dependents_of('a') includes NodeId::Resolution(R0). Fails because build_from_graph doesn't iterate resolutions.

Add resolution iteration to ReverseDependencyIndex::build_from_graph(): iterate graph.resolutions, for each resolution node create NodeId::Resolution(rnode.id.clone()), register each auto_param as a dependency. Also add resolution iteration to build_trace_map(): for each resolution node, create DependencyTrace with reads = auto_params.clone().

Write test in deps.rs: `build_trace_map_includes_resolution`. Build graph with param 'a', 'b' and ResolutionNodeData R0 with auto_params=['a','b']. Call build_trace_map. Assert traces contains NodeId::Resolution(R0) with reads=['a','b'].
Should pass from step 12. Verify and fix if needed.

Write test in demand.rs: `rebuild_cone_includes_resolution_and_deps`. Build graph with: param 'a' (auto), param 'b', constraint C0 reading 'a', ResolutionNodeData R0 with auto_params=['a']. Demand R0, rebuild_cone. Assert R0 is demanded and param 'a' is demanded (transitive through resolution). Assert param 'b' is NOT demanded.
Should pass from step 6's demand.rs match arm addition. Verify the NodeId::Resolution arm in rebuild_cone correctly extracts auto_params and enqueues them. Fix if needed.

Write test in dirty.rs: `dirty_cone_includes_resolution_node`. Build graph with: param 'a', ResolutionNodeData R0 with auto_params=['a']. Build reverse index. Change 'a'. Assert dirty cone contains NodeId::Resolution(R0).

Should pass from step 12 (build_from_graph registers reverse edges from auto_params to Resolution nodes). Dirty cone BFS propagates from changed 'a' → dependents include Resolution(R0). Verify and fix if needed.

Write failing test in graph.rs: `topology_fingerprint_includes_resolutions`. Build two graphs from same templates. Add a ResolutionNodeData to one. Assert fingerprints differ. Also build two graphs with identical ResolutionNodeData, assert fingerprints equal. Fails because topology_fingerprint doesn't hash resolutions.

Add resolution hash computation to topology_fingerprint(): `let res_hash = { let mut hashes: Vec<ContentHash> = self.resolutions.iter().map(|(_, n)| n.content_hash).collect(); hashes.sort_by_key(|h| h.0); ContentHash::combine_all(hashes) };` Update final combine to: `ContentHash::combine_all([vc_hash, cn_hash, real_hash, res_hash])`.

Write test in graph.rs: `fingerprint_domain_separates_resolution_from_others`. Create graph_a with value_cell of hash H, graph_d with resolution of hash H. Assert fingerprints differ. Extends existing domain separation test pattern.
Should pass from step 20 since resolution hashes occupy their own slot in combine_all. Verify.

Write test in graph.rs: `evaluation_graph_resolution_clone_independence`. Insert ResolutionNodeData into graph.resolutions, clone, insert another into clone, verify original has 1 resolution and clone has 2. Matches existing clone_independence test pattern.
Should pass from step 4 since PersistentMap provides structural sharing. Verify.
Run `cargo test --workspace` to verify all 315+ existing tests pass and all new tests pass. No regressions from NodeId variant addition.

Fix any remaining compilation errors or test failures across the workspace. The runtime crate only constructs NodeId variants in tests (no match arms), so it should compile without changes. Any other crates referencing NodeId will need inspection.

design_decisions.decision: NodeId stays in cache.rs rather than moving to reify-types
design_decisions.rationale: The task description mentions extending NodeId in reify-types/identity.rs, but NodeId is actually defined in reify-eval/src/cache.rs. Moving it would be a larger refactor outside this task's scope. We extend it in place.

design_decisions.decision: ResolutionNodeData stores auto_params as Vec<ValueCellId> and constraint_deps as Vec<ConstraintNodeId> (static, not runtime-traced)
design_decisions.rationale: Per task description: 'These dependencies are static (from the template), not dynamically traced.' This is simpler and matches the template-driven design.
A resolution node resolves multiple auto parameters simultaneously. The cache result holds all resolved values keyed by ValueCellId, matching the SolveResult::Solved{values} type in reify-types.

design_decisions.decision: Content hash tag byte [23] for Resolution CachedResult
design_decisions.rationale: Existing tags are [20] Value, [21] Satisfaction, [22] GeometryHandle. Next sequential is [23], maintaining domain separation.

design_decisions.decision: Resolution nodes are leaf nodes in dirty cone propagation
design_decisions.rationale: Like Constraint and Realization nodes, Resolution nodes don't propagate further in the dirty BFS. The resolved values are written back to auto param ValueCells by the engine (not through the dependency graph edges). The dirty.rs compute_dirty_cone only propagates through NodeId::Value, so Resolution nodes naturally become leaves.

design_decisions.decision: from_templates() does not create ResolutionNodes yet
design_decisions.rationale: TopologyTemplate has no auto_params/resolution fields. Compiler changes to detect auto params and emit resolution data in TopologyTemplate are out of scope. Tests construct ResolutionNodes manually by inserting into graph.resolutions.

design_decisions.decision: DemandRegistry::rebuild_cone Resolution arm uses auto_params as dependency reads
design_decisions.rationale: A ResolutionNode's backward dependencies for demand cone BFS are its auto_param ValueCells. These are the inputs the solver reads. Constraint deps (ConstraintNodeId) could also be added as NodeId::Constraint entries to the demand cone queue, but since the demand cone BFS only enqueues NodeId::Value entries for continued traversal, constraint deps would just be inserted as demanded without further propagation — which may be handled later when resolution is fully integrated.

reuse.how: Already defined with new(), Display, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash. Import and use directly via reify_types::ResolutionNodeId.
Use PersistentMap<ResolutionNodeId, ResolutionNodeData> for the new resolutions field, matching existing value_cells/constraints/realizations pattern.
Follow exact pattern: tag byte + combine for CachedResult; sorted hashes + combine_all for topology_fingerprint.
Add identical From<ResolutionNodeId> impl following the three existing implementations.
Add 4th iteration block for graph.resolutions following constraints/realizations pattern but using auto_params directly instead of extract_dependency_trace.
Use existing builder for params/constraints, then manually insert ResolutionNodeData into the graph.resolutions map for tests.

reuse.what: Existing test patterns (construction, clone independence, domain separation)
reuse.how: Follow identical test structure for ResolutionNodeData: field access tests, graph insert/get, clone independence, fingerprint domain separation.
